preamble: The shift from a mid-back rounded nasalized vowel [õ] to a mid-front unrounded nasalized vowel [ẽ] involves unrounding and fronting, often seen as part of a general fronting of back vowels.

examples.note: Fronting of nasalized vowels in various historical contexts.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
